title: New animal welfare NPC hosts first fundraiser
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - Salomé Bruyns’ new local animal welfare organisation, Sentiara, will be hosting its first-ever fundraising event at Her Empire at 10:00 on Saturday 26 July, and everyone is invited.


> Sentiara is the brainchild of senior inspector Bruyns, a well-known figure in George with over 20 years of experience in animal welfare.


Passionate about education, outreach and promoting responsible pet ownership, Bruyns recently launched Sentiara as a non-profit company to expand her efforts and reach more communities.

Guests attending this special fundraiser can look forward to a heartwarming afternoon with a delicious meal and pudding, along with the chance to meet Stevie Wonder, Sentiara’s blind rescue dog, who has a remarkable story of his own. His journey will be shared with guests as part of the day’s inspiring programme.

“We invite people to be part of the change by supporting a cause that truly matters,” says Bruyns.

All proceeds will support Sentiara’s mission to create a lasting impact through education and proactive animal care initiatives.

To book your seat, contact Rika on 082 308 6862. Spaces are limited.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
